focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Babe impresses Mac with her string trick, reigniting his confidence; she secures a paid vacation and promises a front-page story after lunch, leaving Mac hopeful.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Babe demonstrates her string trick to regain Mac's attention, then secures a paid month-long vacation in exchange for promising a front-page story upon her return.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: secure paid time off and front-page control
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Babe has regained Mac's confidence and editorial leverage, with a vacation approved and space reserved on the front page for her next story
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how she will produce the promised front-page story
evidence_quotes: She has a string in her hand which she keeps flicking, trying to get a knot into it—in the manner of cowboys with a rope.
Just as the last one is approaching, she succeeds in doing the trick.
BABE Can I have a month's vacation?
MAC With pay!

relation_updates: renews trust with Mac

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Babe feigns weakness after Longfellow finds her; she refuses his help, claims to have found a job, then pretends to collapse again, prompting him to call a taxi.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Babe fakes fainting and physical weakness to manipulate Longfellow into showing concern, refusing his help while implying hardship, then pretends to collapse again to prompt him to call a taxi.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: establish emotional connection with Longfellow
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Babe is now personally known to Longfellow under false pretenses
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how long she can maintain the deception
evidence_quotes: a girl emerges from the shadows and staggers forward
She reaches a tree and clutches it weakly before collapsing to the ground
she looks up at him with feigned bewilderment, pretending to regain consciousness
she again pretends to weaken, falling against the iron fence and clutching it for support

relation_updates: creates false vulnerability to initiate bond with Longfellow
status_updates: successfully inserts herself into Longfellow’s world

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow shares a vision of Grant's journey from farm boy to president, symbolizing American possibility, moving Babe who refrains from laughing.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Babe refrains from mocking Longfellow's idealistic view of Grant's Tomb and is visibly affected by his sincere expression of American possibility.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Babe begins to regard Longfellow with genuine interest and respect, moving beyond her earlier attitude of detached skepticism.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: her internal conflict between journalistic detachment and growing personal connection
evidence_quotes: BABE (looks up at him): Now, what do you see?
During his recital, she watches his face, fascinated. Her impulse is to laugh, but she finds that she can't.
LONGFELLOW (overcome - he almost chokes on his final words): Excuse me!

relation_updates: shift from skepticism to emotional openness toward Longfellow

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Babe challenges Longfellow to sing 'Humoresque' in counterpoint to her 'Swanee River'; he confidently agrees, and they perform together as she warns him to deliver.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Babe shares memories of her father and small-town upbringing, drawing a parallel between him and Longfellow.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: moving from professional distance to personal connection
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Babe momentarily sets aside her reporter role and speaks openly about her past, establishing emotional common ground with Longfellow.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how her dual role as reporter and confidante will resolve
evidence_quotes: I'm from a small town too, you know.
Ah, it's a beautiful little town, too. A row of poplar trees right along Main Street. Always smelled as if it just had a bath.
I used to love to go fishing with my father. That's funny. He was a lot like you, my father was. Talked like you, too.
He played in the town band, too.

relation_updates: mutual recognition of shared small-town background
emotional bonding through family memories

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 41、INT. LIVING ROOM OF MABEL DAWSON'S STUDIO
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Babe struggles to write, visibly distressed, until a call from the 'Cinderella Man' grabs her attention, prompting her to abruptly take the phone from Mabel.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Babe struggles to write her article on Longfellow, showing visible distress and emotional paralysis as she confronts her discomfort with sensationalizing him.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to complete the assigned article
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Babe is unable to continue writing and expresses frustration, indicating emotional conflict over her task
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether she will proceed with the exploitative story or respond to her growing personal connection to Longfellow
evidence_quotes: CAMERA PULLS BACK and we find Babe, staring at the sheet of paper in front of her.
who at the moment rests her forehead on the typewriter carriage.
BABE (jumping up): I can't write it, Mabel! I don't know what's the matter with me.
MABEL: You haven't gotten very far, have you? That's where you were an hour ago.

relation_updates: increasing emotional distance from her journalistic role regarding Longfellow

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Media headlines mockingly depict the 'Cinderella Man' as a fire-eating demon and link him to opera reform and social ambitions, blending satire with public spectacle.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Babe reflects on Longfellow's sincerity and questions her role in ridiculing him, recognizing a moral contrast between his integrity and her cynical world.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Babe questions her journalistic mission and begins to feel guilt over manipulating Longfellow.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Babe is emotionally unsettled by Longfellow's authenticity and her own complicity in his public mockery.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: She doesn't know whether to continue the deception or protect him.
evidence_quotes: He said when he gets married he wants to carry his bride over the threshold in his arms.
I tried to laugh, but I couldn't. It stuck in my throat.
He's got goodness, Mabel. Do you know what that is?
I'm crucifying him.

relation_updates: Babe sees Longfellow as morally superior to her world
status_updates: Babe's professional cynicism starts to crack

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Babe refuses to quit despite Mac's pleas; he predicts she'll want to return soon, then hands her a phone call, leaving her response uncertain.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Babe formally resigns from her job by handing over her contact book and rejecting Mac's pleas to stay.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: leave her reporting career behind
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Babe has physically left her position and severed professional ties with her editor.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether she will return to journalism or reconcile with Longfellow
evidence_quotes: This is for you, Mac,' she says, explaining that it contains the names of all the headwaiters in town
Mac protests, urging her not to quit, insisting she can't leave now and questioning whether she's truly going through with her decision.
Babe responds with a firm, final shake of her head, signaling her resolve.
Babe takes the phone and says, 'Hello...'

relation_updates: defies Mac's expectations
ends professional alliance with Mac

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A guard blocks Babe from seeing a man who reacts strongly when her name is mentioned, despite her persistent pleas.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Babe learns that mentioning her name triggers a negative emotional reaction in Deeds, revealing the harm her presence causes.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: desperately wants to see Deeds and make contact
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Babe understands that her connection to Deeds now causes him distress rather than comfort
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether she can redeem herself in Deeds' eyes
evidence_quotes: Babe winces under the blow.

relation_updates: learns her presence destabilizes Deeds emotionally
status_updates: experiences visible emotional pain upon hearing the truth

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow defends his plan to give land and supplies to farmers, accuses Cedar of hypocrisy, and punches him in court after accusing him of lying, causing chaos.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Babe admits in court to writing defamatory articles about Longfellow, explains she stopped after recognizing his sincerity, and confirms under pressure that she loves him.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to defend Longfellow's character and retract her role in ridiculing him
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: publicly aligned with Longfellow, emotionally exposed, and morally rehabilitated
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Longfellow will respond to her feelings
evidence_quotes: Certainly I wrote those articles.
I was going to get a raise - and a month's vacation!
But I stopped writing them when I found out what he was all about!
Yes!!!

relation_updates: publicly acknowledges romantic love for Longfellow

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow, declared sane, is celebrated by a mob; Babe is separated but reunited when he returns, kissing passionately as the crowd cheers outside.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After being separated from Longfellow by the celebrating crowd, Babe is left alone in the courtroom until he returns, runs to her, and they embrace and kiss passionately.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to reunite with Longfellow after his acquittal
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: physically reunited with Longfellow and emotionally reassured through mutual affection
evidence_quotes: As Babe frantically tries to reach Longfellow, but is jostled aside.
FULL SHOT OF COURTROOM Empty - except for the Falkner sisters, still whispering to each other, and Babe, sitting helpless and forlorn.
He runs toward her, and swoops her up in his arms.
She kisses him over and over again.

relation_updates: romantic relationship consummated through mutual affection
physical reunion after emotional separation
status_updates: transforms from isolated onlooker to central figure in celebration

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Babe begins as a cynical, ambitious reporter manipulating Longfellow Deeds for a story, using deception to infiltrate his life. As she spends time with him, her professional detachment erodes, replaced by genuine emotional connection and moral conflict. She ultimately confronts her complicity in his public humiliation, quits her job, and publicly confesses her role to redeem herself. The arc culminates in her emotional reconciliation with Longfellow, shifting from manipulator to advocate and, finally, to a loved and accepted partner.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The agent departs, leaving Cobb and Cedar glaring, as the scene transitions.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: John Cedar begins his inquiry into Longfellow Deeds' whereabouts upon arriving in Mandrake Falls, expressing doubt about the mission's validity and encountering uncooperative locals.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: locate Longfellow Deeds to deliver inheritance news
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: mission is underway but hindered by evasive responses from townspeople
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: difficulty obtaining direct information about Deeds from local residents
evidence_quotes: CEDAR Are you sure this is the town he lives in?
CEDAR Well, I dropped everything at the office - I hope it's not a wild goose chase.
ANDERSON No, sir. We checked it thoroughly. He lives here all right.
CEDAR I say, my friend, do you know a fellow by the name of Longfellow Deeds?

relation_updates: reinforces cooperative dynamic with Anderson
establishes shared purpose with Cobb
status_updates: arrives in Mandrake Falls as an outsider
begins active inquiry into Deeds' whereabouts

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow Deeds learns he inherited $20 million from his uncle, reacts calmly, and remains unfazed, pondering why he was chosen despite not needing the money.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: John Cedar identifies himself and his firm to Longfellow Deeds and begins the process of confirming Deeds' identity in person.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: confirm Deeds' identity and prepare to deliver inheritance information
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Cedar has formally introduced himself and his associates to Deeds, establishing a professional presence in Deeds' home
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Deeds has not yet been informed of the inheritance, and his reaction remains unknown
evidence_quotes: CEDAR (standing - formally) Mr. Longfellow Deeds?
LONGFELLOW Yes.
CEDAR How do you do.
CEDAR (extending card) I'm John Cedar - of the New York firm of Cedar, Cedar, Cedar and Budington.

relation_updates: establishes professional rapport with Longfellow Deeds
status_updates: enters Deeds' home as an official representative

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow Deeds learns he inherited $20 million from his uncle, reacts calmly, and remains unfazed, pondering why he was chosen despite not needing the money.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Cedar informs Longfellow Deeds that he has inherited approximately $20 million from his late uncle, Martin W. Semple, after confirming his identity and familial connection.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: successfully communicates the inheritance
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Cedar completes the notification, but is left uncertain due to Deeds' unemotional response and immediate return to playing the tuba
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Deeds' calm and detached reaction raises doubts about his understanding of or suitability for handling the inheritance
evidence_quotes: Perhaps you didn't hear what I said, Mr. Deeds! The whole Semple fortune goes to you! $20,000,000!
Oh, yes, I heard you all right. $20,000,000. That's quite a lot, isn't it?
I wonder why he left me all that money? I don't need it.

relation_updates: establishes authority as the legal messenger
creates dependency as Deeds' link to the new reality

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Cedar dismisses concerns over missing financial documents and a half-million dollars, asserting control and claiming he will regain the Power of Attorney, while his associates react with worry and disbelief.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: central confrontation of risk and control
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Cedar admits he did not secure the Power of Attorney but projects confidence, dismisses financial concerns, and asserts his ability to regain control, silencing worried associates.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: regain Power of Attorney and prevent financial exposure
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Cedar retains leadership position and calms immediate unrest, though the Power of Attorney remains unsecured and financial vulnerabilities persist
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Power of Attorney not obtained, half-million dollars unaccounted for, financial books at risk of exposure
evidence_quotes: Cedar barges in.
CEDAR (beaming) Good morning. Hello, boys.
CEDAR (interrupting) No, Budington, I didn't get the Power of Attorney. But don't worry, I will.

relation_updates: asserts authority over Budington
reinforces alliance with brothers
status_updates: position of control reaffirmed despite setback

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow learns of Mrs. Semple, his uncle's common-law wife with a child, and insists she receive the estate, dismissing his own claim and prioritizing her care over legal disputes.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Longfellow learns that his uncle had a common-law wife, Mrs. Semple, and a child, and immediately declares that she should inherit the entire estate, rejecting his own claim.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: ensure Mrs. Semple and the child are cared for without legal conflict
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Longfellow positions himself as the moral arbiter of the estate, overriding legal concerns raised by Cedar
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the legal disposition of the estate remains undetermined
evidence_quotes: A child? My uncle's?
That's awful. The poor woman should be taken care of immediately.
If she was his wife, she should have all the money. That's only fair. I don't want a penny of it.

relation_updates: rejects Cedar's legalistic approach
dismisses Hallor as untrustworthy
auxiliary.persona_anchor: moral clarity in contrast to Cedar's legalism

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Cedar, intrigued by Semples' claim of nuisance value, abruptly invites them in after dismissing relatives, signaling a shift in his approach to their persistent demands.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Cedar shifts from dismissing the Semple relatives to recognizing their potential utility after hearing the phrase 'nuisance value,' and abruptly invites them into his office.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: exploit legal vulnerabilities to gain control
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: begins direct engagement with the Semples as possible allies against Deeds
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Power of Attorney still not secured
evidence_quotes: Relatives of old man Semple.
They keep insisting they should have some nuisance value.

relation_updates: engages Semple relatives as potential allies

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Semple reluctantly signs the document under pressure from Cedar and his wife; Cedar activates a recording device, confirms the papers are set, and orders an investigation into the author of newspaper articles.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Cedar pressures Semple to sign a legal agreement, activates a recording device, and initiates an investigation into the authors of newspaper articles supporting Deeds.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seize control of Semple fortune by discrediting Deeds
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: legal action against Deeds is formally initiated and investigative efforts are underway
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: public opinion remains a threat
evidence_quotes: Cedar shoves a paper in front of Semple.
Simultaneously, Cedar flicks a button on his dictagraph.
Find out who wrote those newspaper articles and subpoena them right away.

relation_updates: formalizes alliance with Semple
asserts authority over legal team

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow remains silent and unresponsive in court, refusing counsel as the judge warns against disturbances and proceeds with the sanity hearing despite his lack of defense.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: lead attorney initiating the sanity hearing against Longfellow Deeds
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Cedar organizes his legal team and evidence in court, preparing to advance the case for Longfellow's incompetence without opposition.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To prove Longfellow Deeds is mentally unfit to manage the inheritance
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Cedar is positioned as the active legal authority in the hearing, with the court proceeding in the absence of a defense
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether the court will rule in Cedar's favor given Longfellow's continued silence and lack of legal representation
evidence_quotes: Cedar and his assistants arrange their papers.
SEMPLE (to Cedar) Here he is!

relation_updates: establishes adversarial stance toward Longfellow Deeds
status_updates: formally leading the legal proceedings in court

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow defends his plan to give land and supplies to farmers, accuses Cedar of hypocrisy, and punches him in court after accusing him of lying, causing chaos.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: lead counsel arguing for Longfellow's incompetence
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Cedar presents a detailed case that Longfellow's behavior is erratic and dangerous, citing media reports, witness accounts, and public order concerns to justify legal intervention.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To convince the court that Longfellow is mentally unfit to manage the Semple fortune
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Cedar has positioned Longfellow as a threat to social stability, framing the hearing as a necessary safeguard against his unpredictable actions
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Babe Bennett's testimony will contradict or support Cedar's portrayal of Longfellow's behavior
evidence_quotes: She tells how, in the midst of a normal conversation, he would suddenly begin playing his tuba.
She tells of his attacks upon several of our eminent writers - for no apparent reason.

relation_updates: intensifies opposition to Longfellow Deeds
aligns with Semple and psychiatrists against Longfellow
status_updates: establishes narrative of Longfellow as a public threat
auxiliary.persona_anchor: opponent of Longfellow's idealism, aligned with institutional authority

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow defends his plan to give land and supplies to farmers, accuses Cedar of hypocrisy, and punches him in court after accusing him of lying, causing chaos.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Cedar calls Babe Bennett to authenticate articles depicting Longfellow as unstable, but she rejects their truthfulness, stating they were written to make him look silly.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To validate the articles as truthful documentation of Longfellow's instability
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Cedar's attempt to introduce the articles as evidence is undermined by Babe's public retraction and accusation of bias
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether the court will still admit the articles as evidence despite Babe's retraction
evidence_quotes: Your Honor, at this time, we would like to call our first witness: Miss Louise - Babe -- Bennett.
CEDAR Did you subsequently write a series of articles about him?
BABE NO!!
They're colored! Just to make him look silly!

relation_updates: confronts Babe Bennett as a hostile witness
reveals dependence on media portrayal to sustain case

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow defends his plan to give land and supplies to farmers, accuses Cedar of hypocrisy, and punches him in court after accusing him of lying, causing chaos.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: counsel facing turning point in trial
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Cedar's objections are overruled as witnesses and spectators express support for Longfellow, leading to Longfellow deciding to speak in court.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to prevent Longfellow from regaining agency
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: legal advantage eroding due to emotional and public pressure
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Longfellow is about to take the stand and speak for himself
evidence_quotes: But Your Honor - this is preposterous!
The woman's obviously in love with him.
When the windbag here gets through, Your Honor, I'd like to verify what Miss Bennett said.
The clients look

relation_updates: loses authority in front of judge and crowd
status_updates: shifts from confident to visibly concerned

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow, declared sane, is celebrated by a mob; Babe is separated but reunited when he returns, kissing passionately as the crowd cheers outside.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Cedar is visibly stunned and isolated as the case against Longfellow is dismissed, marking the collapse of his legal challenge.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Cedar is discredited and left without influence in the courtroom
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: His standing and future actions after public defeat
evidence_quotes: CLOSE SHOT - CEDAR AND GROUP They sit, stunned, disappointed.
Budington rises to confront Cedar.
Cedar disgustedly pushes him in the face, aside.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: John Cedar begins as a determined legal representative tasked with delivering life-changing news to Longfellow Deeds, but quickly shifts from facilitator to antagonist as he seeks to control the inheritance. His skepticism of Deeds' stability evolves into a calculated legal campaign to have him declared incompetent, culminating in a courtroom showdown. When Deeds defends his ideals and exposes Cedar's manipulations, Cedar's authority collapses, leaving him discredited and defeated.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow Deeds learns he inherited $20 million from his uncle, reacts calmly, and remains unfazed, pondering why he was chosen despite not needing the money.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Longfellow learns he has inherited $20 million from his uncle and reacts with minimal emotion, expressing curiosity about why he was chosen despite not needing the money.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally unfazed by sudden wealth
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: unclear why his uncle chose him for the inheritance
evidence_quotes: Gee, that's too bad. If there's anything I can do to
Oh, yes, I heard you all right. $20,000,000. That's quite a lot, isn't it?
I wonder why he left me all that money? I don't need it.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: values simplicity and community over wealth

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow learns he's one of the richest men in the country and must travel to New York; he's nervous about leaving Mandrake Falls for the first time.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Longfellow is informed he must travel to New York for the first time and reacts with nervousness, though expresses curiosity about seeing Grant's Tomb.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: must go to New York
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: anxious but compliant about leaving Mandrake Falls
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: fear of leaving familiar environment

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow departs on a train, waving goodbye to a cheering crowd as the band plays; he expresses gratitude for his many friends, moved by the farewell.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Longfellow is visibly moved by the enthusiastic farewell from his community, expressing surprise and appreciation as he waves goodbye and acknowledges the number of friends he has.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally affirmed by community support, departing with tangible tokens of affection
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: transition from small-town simplicity to complex city life
evidence_quotes: In the f.g. the crowd yells its farewell.
Several of them stuff baskets of fruit into his hands.
Longfellow smiles wanly and waves.
Gosh, I've got a lot of friends.

Cobb visibly affected, indicating growing emotional investment
status_updates: in possession of tuba and gifts from townspeople

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow learns of Mrs. Semple, his uncle's common-law wife with a child, and insists she receive the estate, dismissing his own claim and prioritizing her care over legal disputes.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Upon learning that his uncle had a common-law wife and child, Longfellow immediately states that the woman should receive the estate and that he does not want any of the money, prioritizing their care over legal claims.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: prioritizes care for others over legal ownership
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: declines personal claim to the inheritance and directs it toward Mrs. Semple and her child
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: legal and social consequences of rejecting inheritance
evidence_quotes: What kind of wife did you say she was?
A child? My uncle's?
That's awful. The poor woman should be taken care of immediately.
If she was his wife, she should have all the money. That's only fair. I don't want a penny of it.

relation_updates: defies Cedar's legal guidance
asserts moral authority over estate
status_updates: learns of Mrs. Semple and child
auxiliary.persona_anchor: acts on principle, not legal convenience

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow Deeds asserts the opera house must operate as a business to avoid $180,000 losses, exits defiantly after challenging the board, leaving them stunned, while Cobb adds a mocking remark.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: defiant rejection of board's financial expectations and exit
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Longfellow insists the opera must operate as a business, refuses to cover the $180,000 loss without scrutiny, and leaves the meeting, asserting control over his resources.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: protecting his resources to do good on his own terms
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: exits the board meeting, no longer participating in the opera's management, and distances himself from the board's approach
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how he will use his wealth in a way that aligns with his ideals
evidence_quotes: Well, maybe it isn't to you, but it certainly is a business to me, if I have to make up a loss of $180,000.
I expect to do a lot of good with that money. And I can't afford to put it into anything that I don't look into.
That's my decision for the time being, gentlemen. Goodbye, and thank you for making me Chairman.
He exits, followed by Cobb, whose eyes shriek his admiration.

relation_updates: solidifies adversarial relationship with board
strengthens bond with Cobb through shared defiance
auxiliary.persona_anchor: rejects institutional practices he sees as financially irresponsible and demands accountability

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow rejects Cobb's offer of women for entertainment, expressing discomfort; he questions Walter about his uncle's rumored affairs, learning he once hosted up to twenty women, though their purpose remains unclear.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: rejecting decadence and seeking moral clarity
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Longfellow declines Cobb's offer of women for entertainment and expresses discomfort with the casual treatment of women, reflecting his disapproval of the lifestyle associated with his uncle's wealth.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to understand his uncle's past while distancing himself from corrupt habits
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: morally unsettled by the hedonistic environment surrounding his inheritance
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: uncertainty about his uncle's true character and behavior
evidence_quotes: Some other time, Cobb. Some other time.
He talks about women as if they were cattle.
Tell me, Walter, are all those stories I hear about my uncle true?
Well, sir, he sometimes had as many as twenty in the house at the same time.

relation_updates: disgusted by Cobb's values
auxiliary.persona_anchor: maintains personal integrity in a corrupt environment

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow shares a vision of Grant's journey from farm boy to president, symbolizing American possibility, moving Babe who refrains from laughing.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Longfellow describes Grant's rise from farm boy to president as a uniquely American possibility, affecting Babe's perception of him.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to communicate belief in democratic ideals and personal transformation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Babe is visibly moved and refrains from laughing, indicating a shift from mockery to quiet respect.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: his idealism contrasts with the public's cynicism and Babe's initial skepticism
evidence_quotes: LONGFELLOW: That depends on what they see.

relation_updates: Babe refrains from mocking him, marking a shift in her attitude from skepticism to respect
auxiliary.persona_anchor: believes in the dignity of ordinary people and self-made success

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow, initially cheerful, is shattered upon learning from Cobb that Babe Bennett, not Mary Dawson, manipulated him for a story, revealing her Pulitzer win and his public humiliation as the 'Cinderella Man'.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: emotional devastation and narrative turning point
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Longfellow learns Babe Bennett, not Mary Dawson, manipulated him for a story, revealing her identity as a reporter who exposed him as the 'Cinderella Man,' leading to his public and personal humiliation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To prepare for a romantic meeting with Babe; shifts to shock and denial upon learning of her deception.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Longfellow is emotionally shattered and withdrawn, collapsing onto the bed in disbelief after confronting the evidence of Babe's betrayal.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How Longfellow will respond to the betrayal and whether he can recover from the personal and public exposure.
evidence_quotes: Longfellow exclaims, 'Anything happened? I've got to get dressed! I can't meet her like this!'
Devastated, he staggers to the bed and collapses, still staring at the picture.

relation_updates: romantic relationship revealed as deception
Babe redefined as antagonist from Longfellow's perspective
auxiliary.persona_anchor: vulnerable to emotional manipulation due to idealism

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow Deeds' plan to give away his fortune by creating farms sparks national attention, stunning elites, prompting investigations, and drawing thousands seeking a new life.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Longfellow decides to donate his fortune to create self-sustaining farms for the unemployed, following his compassionate interaction with a struggling man.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to give away his fortune by creating self-sustaining farms for the unemployed
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Longfellow becomes the center of national attention as his plan triggers public response and logistical efforts to process applicants.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how the farm distribution will be managed and whether the plan can be successfully implemented amid high demand.
evidence_quotes: The man speaks tentatively, asking if he can take some of the food home
Longfellow silently nods in response

relation_updates: extends compassion to the man at the table
auxiliary.persona_anchor: responds to personal encounter with concrete, large-scale action

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow defends his plan to give land and supplies to farmers, accuses Cedar of hypocrisy, and punches him in court after accusing him of lying, causing chaos.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Deeds interrupts the hearing to challenge false claims about his behavior and defend his plan to help farmers
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Deeds stands and confronts Cedar, rejecting the portrayal of his actions as signs of insanity and accusing Cedar of lying, then punches him in the courtroom
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to defend his character and the legitimacy of his plan
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Deeds breaks his silence and physically attacks Cedar, causing chaos in the courtroom
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether the court and public will interpret his outburst as a sign of madness or justified outrage
evidence_quotes: She tells of his attacks upon several of our eminent writers - for no apparent reason.

relation_updates: Deeds confronts Cedar as antagonist
Public perception begins to shift as truth emerges
status_updates: Deeds transitions from passive to active resistance
auxiliary.persona_anchor: responds directly when personally attacked or when his values are misrepresented

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow defends his plan to give land and supplies to farmers, accuses Cedar of hypocrisy, and punches him in court after accusing him of lying, causing chaos.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: defendant asserting rationality through observed behaviors
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Longfellow counters claims of insanity by pointing out common idiosyncratic behaviors in authority figures, using humor and direct observation to challenge their credibility.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to demonstrate that personal quirks do not constitute insanity
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Longfellow shifts courtroom perception by highlighting the normalcy of odd habits, temporarily undermining the medical and judicial authority of his evaluators
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether the court will legally accept behavioral normalcy as evidence against a diagnosis of insanity
evidence_quotes: Dr. Von Holler, here, could probably think up a long name for it, because he doodles all the time.
(scrutinizes it) I can't figure it out. One minute it looks like a chimpanzee - and the next minute it looks like a picture of Mr. Cedar.

relation_updates: Judge becomes a target of gentle humor but remains engaged
Dr. Von Holler is subtly discredited as equally quirky
status_updates: Longfellow gains rhetorical control of the courtroom
auxiliary.persona_anchor: uses plain-spoken observation and humor to level the power imbalance in formal settings

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Longfellow, declared sane, is celebrated by a mob; Babe is separated but reunited when he returns, kissing passionately as the crowd cheers outside.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Declared sane by the court, Longfellow is carried out by a cheering crowd but returns to find Babe, reuniting with her in a passionate embrace.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Recognized as sane and celebrated by the public, yet chooses to return for Babe, reaffirming their relationship.
evidence_quotes: Immediately, Longfellow is surrounded by a crowd of people who come running down the aisles.
At that moment, several farmers have lifted Longfellow on their shoulders.
He reaches courtroom, slams the doors shut behind him.
He runs toward her, and swoops her up in his arms.

relation_updates: romantic bond with Babe reaffirmed through reunion and passionate kiss
status_updates: elevated to folk hero status by farmers and crowd
auxiliary.persona_anchor: values personal connection over public acclaim

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Longfellow Deeds, a humble tuba-playing poet from Mandrake Falls, inherits a $20 million fortune and is thrust into the complexities of urban wealth and power. Initially detached from the money, he resists corruption, rejects decadence, and seeks moral clarity, only to be manipulated and publicly ridiculed. After being betrayed by Babe, the reporter he loved, he withdraws in despair but finds renewed purpose in helping the unemployed. His attempt to give away his fortune leads to an insanity hearing, where he defiantly defends his values and is ultimately vindicated, reclaiming both his sanity and love.
